issue_title: Entities with 'state_class: total_increasing' generate a warning if they get a non-numeric state like "unknown" or "unavailable"
issue_body:
### The problem

In 2021.9b0, two of my entities with `state_class: total_increasing` had a single instance of a non-numeric state, and that caused the following log message for each:

`2021-08-25 15:12:01 WARNING (Recorder) [homeassistant.components.sensor.recorder] Entity sensor.eagle_energy_consumed from integration mqtt has state class total_increasing, but its state is not strictly increasing. Please create a bug report at https://github.com/home-assistant/core/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22integration%3A+recorder%22`

The numeric values for the state of each entity are monotonically increasing, and chat in the Discord beta channel concluded that the non-numeric state shouldn't cause that log warning.

Here are examples of the state data for the two entities:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/860888/130874841-df20e939-adac-420d-96f3-8e0419a33f49.png)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/860888/130874867-0ea0c7a4-7b3d-4cad-9f69-1296422422f6.png)


### What is version of Home Assistant Core has the issue?

core-2021.9.0b0

### What was the last working version of Home Assistant Core?

N/A

### What type of installation are you running?

Home Assistant OS

### Integration causing the issue

recorder

### Link to integration documentation on our website

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/recorder/

### Example YAML snippet

```yaml
N/A
```


### Anything in the logs that might be useful for us?

```txt
2021-08-25 15:12:01 WARNING (Recorder) [homeassistant.components.sensor.recorder] Entity sensor.eagle_energy_consumed from integration mqtt has state class total_increasing, but its state is not strictly increasing. Please create a bug report at https://github.com/home-assistant/core/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22integration%3A+recorder%22
2021-08-25 15:12:01 WARNING (Recorder) [homeassistant.components.sensor.recorder] Entity sensor.garage_pump_switch_electric_kwh from integration zwave_js has state class total_increasing, but its state is not strictly increasing. Please create a bug report at https://github.com/home-assistant/core/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22integration%3A+recorder%22
```
```


### Additional information

_No response_
pr_title: Warn if a sensor with state_class_total has a decreasing value twice
pr_body:
<!--
  You are amazing! Thanks for contributing to our project!
  Please, DO NOT DELETE ANY TEXT from this template! (unless instructed).
-->
## Proposed change
<!--
  Describe the big picture of your changes here to communicate to the
  maintainers why we should accept this pull request. If it fixes a bug
  or resolves a feature request, be sure to link to that issue in the
  additional information section.
-->
Warn if a sensor with state_class_total has a decreasing value twice

Warning after a single decreasing value may trigger false positives due to
rounding issues with databases storing the state as a single precision float, which
was fixed in recorder DB version 20.

Another option would be to consult the recorder's `SchemaChanges` table and suppress warnings
the 1st statistics run after schema migration.

## Type of change
<!--
  What type of change does your PR introduce to Home Assistant?
